original: Landing of the Huygens probe on Saturn's moon Titan
Spinning disk of ice 300m wide, resembling the moon, forms in the Presumpscot river, Westbrook, Maine
John Mallard, English medical physicist (developed MRI and PET scans), born in Northampton, England (d. 2021)
Hugh Lofting, English writer and illustrator (Dr. Doolittle), born in Maidenhead, England (d. 1947)
Researchers in Dallas, Texas present findings about an enzyme that slows aging and cell death (apoptosis)
Dancer Roy Castle is measured at 1,440 taps/min on BBC TV
The musical instrument, the clarinet is invented in Nürnberg, Germany
29-year-old French woman Marie Claude Adam gives birth to sextuplets in Paris
Anita Wold (Norway) sets women's ski jump distance record-98 m
Inna Lassovskaya triple-jumps ladies world record (14.61m)
Roald Amundsen's South Pole expedition lands on the Ross Ice Shelf
MESSENGER spacecraft performs a Mercury flyby
Shannon Lucid, Chinese astronaut (STS 51G, 34, 43, 58, 76/79), born in Shanghai, China
Dr Jesse Bennett performs 1st successful Cesarean section operation in the US, on his wife, Elizabeth, delivering a baby girl at theie home in Edom, Virginia
Russian manned space craft TM-17, lands
Montreal Canadiens' left wing Alex Smart becomes the first NHL rookie to score a hat trick in his first NHL game, a 5-1 win at home over the Chicago Black Hawks
Massachusetts holds day of fasting for wrongly persecuting "witches"
6,960 m (22,834 ft) Cerro Aconcagua (Argentina) 1st climbed by European expedition led by British mountaineer Edward FitzGerald
American inventorEli Whitneyreceives government contract for 10,000 muskets
US Army promotesElvis Presleyto Sergeant
American rockerLittle Richardreleases single "Tutti Frutti"
Ken Sailors, American basketball point guard (popularised and may have invented the jump shot), born in Bushnell, Nebraska (d. 2016)
Utah Jazz center Mark Eaton blocks 2 shots in a 96-89 win over Seattle SuperSonics; becomes only 2nd player in NBA history (along withKareem Abdul-Jabbar) to record 3,000 career blocks
Brock Purdywins his first playoff game as a rookie quarterback against the Seattle Seahawks
Julian Bond, American civil rights leader and politician (D-Ga), born in Nashville Tennessee (d. 2015)
World War II:Franklin D. Roosevelttravels from Miami to Morocco to meet withWinston Churchill, becoming the first American president to travel overseas by airplane
US PresidentJimmy CarterproposesMartin Luther King Jr.'s birthday be a federal holiday
Martina Navratilovais 3rd to win 100 tennis tournaments (Connors & Evert)
Puerto Rican boxer Wilfred Benitez beats WBC welterweight champion Carlos Palomino of Mexico in a 15-round split decision in San Juan, Puerto Rico; becomes two-division champion at the age of 20
Super Bowl VII, LA Memorial Coliseum, Los Angeles, CA: Miami Dolphins beat Washington Redskins, 14-7; MVP: Jake Scott, Miami, S
Super Bowl II, Miami Orange Bowl, Miami, FL: Green Bay Packers beat Oakland Raiders, 33-14; MVP: Bart Starr, Green Bay, QB
John McEnroewins his first of 3 season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix tennis titles 6–7, 6–3, 7–5 against fellow AmericanArthur Asheat Madison Square Garden, NYC
Gerben Karstens, Dutch road cyclist (Olympic gold 100km team trial 1964; 6 x stages Tour de France, 14 x stages Vuelta a España, 1 x stage Giro d'Italia), born in Voorburg, Netherlands (d. 2022)
"Aloha from Hawaii via Satellite", anElvis Presleyconcert broadcast live to Asia and Oceana, and on delay to Europe and US, becomes the most watched broadcast by an individual entertainer
RCA releasesDavid Bowie's 11th studio album, "Low"; the album is the first collaboration with Brian Eno, in what becomes known as his 'Berlin trilogy'
David Bowiereleases his 1st single "Can't Help Thinking About Me"
Pitcher Jim "Catfish" Hunter and outfielder Billy Williams are elected to Baseball Hall of Fame

Vinny Pazienza beats Panamanian boxing legendRoberto Duránin a 12-round unanimous decision in a super middleweight bout in Atlantic City; 2nd time in 6 months Pazienza wears down 43-year-old Duran in a high profile pay-pay-view bout
Jack Taylor, American baseball pitcher (MLB record 187 consecutive complete games; World Series 1907, NL ERA leader 1902 Chicago Cubs), born in New Straitsville, Ohio (d. 1938)
Thornton W. Burgess, American conservationist and author (The Bedtime Story-Books; Now I Remember: Autobiography of an Amateur Naturalist), born in Sandwich, Massachusetts (d. 1965)
Sophie Redmond, Suriname's 1st female physician, born in Paramaribo, Suriname (d. 1955)
Zuzana Ruzickova, Czech harpsichordist and Holocaust survivor, born in Bohemia, Czech Republic (d. 2017)
Krishnadevaraya, Emperor of the Vijayanagara Empire (1509-29), 3rd ruler of the Tuluva dynasty, and one of the greatest rulers in Indian history, born in Vijayanagara Empire (d. 1529)
Martin Niemöller, German theologian who supported, then opposed the Nazi regime, famous for his widely quoted poem "First they came ...", born in Lippstadt, Germany (d. 1984)
Albert Schweitzer, Alsatian theologian and writer (Nobel 1954), born in Kayserberg, France (d. 1965)
Legendary Russian test pilotMarina Popovichis awarded the honorary title "The Hero of the Nation" by the Russian Federation
Margrethe IIof Denmark becomes the first Queen of Denmark since 1412
WolfgangMozartcompletes his String Quartet No. 19 in C ("Dissonance quartet"), the last of six quartet set dedicated toJoseph Haydn(Op. 10/6, K. 465)
"Bonnie Prince Charlie", PrinceCharles Edward Stuart's Jacobite army leaves Glasgow, Scotland [OS=Jan 3]
US Supreme court rules race separation on trains unconstitutional
An earthquake in Kingston, Jamaica, kills more than 1,000
Dutch Zuiderzee dyke cracks
Norway claims Queen Maud Land in Antarctica
Mel Lastman, Mayor of Toronto, calls in the Army to help with snow removal and emergency medical evacuations after major snowstorm hits the city
Frederik X becomes King of Denmark at Christiansborg Castle, Copenhagen, after the abdication of his mother Margrethe at 83, and the world's last reigning queen
The national flag of Georgia, the so-called "five cross flag" restored to official use after a hiatus of some 500 years
UK declared free of foot-and-mouth cattle disease
Cecil Beaton, British photographer and costume designer (My Fair Lady, Gigi), born in Hampstead, England (d. 1980)
Dudley Randall, African-American poet who founded the pioneering publishing house "Broadside Press", born in Washington, D.C. (d. 2000)
Guy Williams [Armando Joseph Catalano], American actor (Zorro; Lost in Space), born in New York City (d. 1989)
